title: Influence Mechanism of Coexisting Ions on the Extraction Efficiency of Lithium from Oil and Gas Field Water
chemistry: HBTA + TOPO + sulfonated kerosene; saponified extractant; conventional non-ionic ligand/synergist system
feed_context: Actual 15 L oil and gas field water process case plus simulated coexisting-ion feed
numeric_claims: Actual process: average Li transfer 91.35%; Li2CO3 purity 99.28%; repo process table: three-stage after impurity removal 97.17% Li extraction; current MS-2 source-regressed Li/Na model: one-stage 47.2846% Li and 0.0131% Na, three-stage model 99.99999% Li and 0.1160% Na with costing capped at 97.17%
reaction_constants_status: No published equilibrium constants found in local metadata/notes; source-regressed stage constants are available but validated K values remain a gap
case_study_role: Flagship non-ionic chemistry and active showcase

title: Recovery of lithium from alkaline brine by solvent extraction with beta-diketone
chemistry: HBTA + TOPO + kerosene; beta-diketone extractant with neutral organophosphorus synergist
numeric_claims: 0.4 mol/L HBTA + 0.3 mol/L TOPO; three theoretical extraction stages for 95%; mixer-settler average Li extraction 96%; stripping solution 15 g/L Li; no third phase or emulsification reported
reaction_constants_status: No direct ePC-SAFT-ready reaction constants found from local metadata; useful for process chemistry and parameter search
case_study_role: Support paper for HBTA/TOPO chemistry, not source-composition anchor

title: Lithium recovery from shale gas produced water using solvent extraction
chemistry: D2EHPA pretreatment plus D2EHPA/TBP second-stage extraction in kerosene
feed_context: Synthetic Marcellus-like shale-gas produced water; Marcellus area Li average about 95 mg/L
numeric_claims: First stage removes more than 94.4% Ca/Mg/Sr/Ba using 1.0 M D2EHPA; second stage reaches 41.2% Li extraction with 1.5 M D2EHPA + 0.3 M TBP; total Li recovered 30.8%; local placeholder crossflow gives about 40.1263% Li and 38.2085% Na after 10 contacts
reaction_constants_status: Paper gives extraction formalism and solvation-number-style analysis, but no directly usable fitted equilibrium constants were confirmed locally

title: Solvent extraction of lithium from brines with high magnesium/lithium ratios: investigation on parameter interactions
feed_context: High Mg/Li brine, not produced-water flagship
numeric_claims: Highest one-stage Li extraction 76.3%; Li/Mg separation factor 304 at Fe/Li 2.99, HCl 0.01 M, TBP 55%; Mg/Li mass ratio reduced from 192 in feed to 1.5 in stripping solution
reaction_constants_status: No direct ePC-SAFT reaction constants found in local metadata

title: Thermodynamic modeling of lithium extraction from synthetic brine using deep eutectic solvents: A PC-SAFT approach
chemistry: TBAC/decanoic-acid DES + TOPO; organic reaction-product pseudo-species
numeric_claims: Reports DES and TOPO PC-SAFT-style parameter values, fitted organic binary interactions, and aqueous ePC-SAFT treatment
reaction_constants_status: Reaction constants are estimated through thermodynamic/group-contribution workflow; not a full HBTA/TOPO ePC-SAFT payload
case_study_role: Parameter/regression smoke-test source only; organic phase is PC-SAFT and aqueous phase is ePC-SAFT

title: Synergistic Deep Eutectic Solvents for Lithium Extraction
chemistry: HBTA/TOPO and HTTA/TOPO deep eutectic solvent chemistry
numeric_claims: Reports HBTA/TOPO and HTTA/TOPO physical properties and strong Li-over-Na/K selectivity
reaction_constants_status: No full ePC-SAFT parameter set or extraction equilibrium constants found yet

title: Selective extraction of lithium from alkaline brine using HBTA-TOPO synergistic extraction system
chemistry: HBTA + TOPO + kerosene; synergistic beta-diketone/neutral phosphine oxide chemistry
numeric_claims: Supports 2 HBTA : 1 TOPO : 1 Li stoichiometry, strong Li/Na selectivity, and multistage extraction/stripping conditions
